perturbation.original_text: Provider may not make any change in the Provider Representatives without the prior consent of the Recipient. Provider Representatives shall be dedicated to solely providing the Services to Recipient and shall not provide any such services or resources to Provider or any other customer of Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: Provider may make changes in the Provider Representatives without the consent of the Recipient. Provider Representatives shall be dedicated to providing the Services to Recipient, and may provide similar services to other customers of Provider.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause requiring prior consent for changes in Provider Representatives and the exclusivity of their services, the modified text contradicts the implied reliance on specific personnel and their sole dedication to the Recipient. This creates uncertainty in service continuity and quality. This contradicts the standard of service originally agreed upon, as now there is no garuantee of the same level of experience or dedication to the reciepient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (a) Provider represents, warrants and agrees that the Services shall be provided in good faith, in accordance with applicable law and in a manner generally consistent with the historical provision of the Services and with the same standard of care as historically provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for prior consent for changes in provider representatives and allows them to provide similar services to other customers. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which warrants that services will be provided in a manner consistent with historical provision and the same standard of care. The change eliminates the guarantee of dedicated personnel and their specific expertise, thus undermining the standard of care historically provided. This creates a direct conflict regarding the expected service quality and continuity.

perturbation.original_text: As consideration for provision of the Services following the Effective Date, Recipient shall pay Provider an amount equal to Provider's actual cost of providing the Services plus a 10% service fee. In addition to such amount, in the event that Provider incurs reasonable and documented out-of-pocket expenses in the provision of any Service, including, without limitation, license fees and payments to third-party service providers or subcontractors (such included expenses, collectively, "Out-of-Pocket Costs"), Recipient shall reimburse Provider for all such Out-of-Pocket Costs.
perturbation.changed_text: As consideration for provision of the Services following the Effective Date, Recipient shall pay Provider an amount to be determined based on market rates. Recipient may, at its discretion, reimburse Provider for certain expenses.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the fixed cost-plus-10% service fee structure and replacing it with 'an amount to be determined based on market rates' and discretionary reimbursement for expenses, the revised text contradicts the original payment terms. This creates ambiguity and potential disputes regarding the compensation owed to Provider. Also, due to 'market rates' being undefined, the clause is ambigious for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Recipient and the Provider may, in their sole discretion, terminate this Agreement in whole or in part, at any time without cause, and without liability except, in the case of the Recipient, for required payment for services rendered and reimbursement for authorized expenses incurred, by providing at least 90 (ninety) days' prior written notice to the other party (such date, the "Services Termination Date").
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a 'cost-plus-10% service fee' and reimbursement for 'Out-of-Pocket Costs.' The changed text replaces this with 'an amount to be determined based on market rates' and discretionary reimbursement. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that 'Recipient' is liable 'for required payment for services rendered and reimbursement for authorized expenses incurred.' The original text made these payments and reimbursements mandatory, while the changed text introduces uncertainty and discretion, contradicting the Recipient's payment obligations outlined in the contradicted text. This is a strong contradiction as the payment terms are fundamentally altered, creating a risk of non-payment where payment was previously guaranteed.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination of this Agreement in its entirety pursuant to Section 3.1, all obligations of the Parties hereto shall terminate, except for the provisions of Section 2.2, and the entirety of Sections 4, 5 and 6, which shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination of this Agreement in its entirety pursuant to Section 3.1, all obligations of the Parties hereto shall terminate, except for Section 4, 5 and 6, which shall survive any termination or expiration of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The removal of the phrase 'the provisions of Section 2.2' means that the payment terms no longer survive termination, this then contradicts the Recipient's payment obligation upon termination, due to the services rendered and reimbursement of authorized expenses. This contradiction creates uncertainty over financial liabilities after the agreement ends.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the Recipient and the Provider may, in their sole discretion, terminate this Agreement in whole or in part, at any time without cause, and without liability except, in the case of the Recipient, for required payment for services rendered and reimbursement for authorized expenses incurred, by providing at least 90 (ninety) days' prior written notice to the other party (such date, the "Services Termination Date").
perturbation.justification: The original text ensures that Section 2.2, relating to payment terms, survives termination. The changed text removes this provision, leading to a direct conflict with the contradicted text. The contradicted text states that the Recipient is liable 'for required payment for services rendered and reimbursement for authorized expenses incurred' even upon termination. By removing Section 2.2 from the list of surviving obligations, the changed text contradicts this payment obligation, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' definition.
